summary: Reality-only review. 0 hallucinations, 0 blocking issues, 3 LOW warnings. Every legis-side symbol, path, response shape, and spec cross-reference confirmed against the codebase. Both Section A gaps (A1 missing rename-feed contract-lock test, A2 status conflation in build_rename_feed) are real and correctly described. Sibling claims in Section B confirmed against local /home/john/clarion and /home/john/filigree copies; Filigree signature column (B1) and gate-call (B5) correctly described as still-open joint steps.

warnings.issue: Plan prose cites 2026-06-05-legis-home-closeout.review.json (source of M5/M6) but that file was removed in the superpowers-artifact cleanup. Labels are genuine (verified in git show 4cb1731); task descriptions are self-contained. A worker should not try to open the file.

'480 passed, 2 skipped' is a time-sensitive baseline; collection currently shows 482 (480 runnable + 2 skip-collected). Math is consistent. Exit criterion is 'green', not a fixed count. Confirm with `uv run pytest -q` before Section A.
tests/git/test_rename_feed.py currently asserts only old_path/new_path of RenameEvidence; A1's full-field contract-lock is correctly identified as new work.

information_gaps: Filigree close_issue/api_close_issue gate-call absence (B5) inferred from home-closeout design doc, not directly read in /home/john/filigree source — verify before coordinating.
Sibling claims verified against local /home/john/clarion and /home/john/filigree copies; ensure those are current before acting on Section B.

risk.reversibility: Easy — Section A tasks are additive; /git/renames contract and Clarion consumer untouched.

next_steps: Section A is safe to execute (run A1 contract-lock before A2 so the committed shape is pinned first). Sections B-D are coordination/tracking; their exits live in sibling repos.
